issues: The drafted headline is a paraphrase, not the actual headline from the OCR. No clear bold headline is present in the OCR text — the article begins directly with the byline.

The long description omits key details: Red Deer fans threw eggs, tomatoes, and fish after every Rustler goal; one Richmond player was hit in the face by an egg; two Red Deer players received suspensions (1 and 3 games) for a post-game altercation with fans in Richmond the previous week; Richmond was fined $500 by the CAHA for failing to provide proper security at that game.

The long description incorrectly characterises the bench-clearing incident — the OCR describes a 'fracas behind the Richmond Sockeyes players bench instigated by disorderly fans,' not a bench-clearing incident per se.
The series is tied 2-2 in a best-of-seven (Game 4 won 7-1 by Red Deer), which is not mentioned in the draft.
review.correctedByline: By JACK WILSON of The Advocate
review.correctedDescriptionShort: CAHA warns the Alberta-B.C. junior championship between the Red Deer Rustlers and Richmond Sockeyes could be cancelled or moved unless fans stop throwing debris and eggs at players.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Canadian Amateur Hockey Association warned that unruly fan behaviour at Red Deer Arena has jeopardized the best-of-seven junior championship series between the Red Deer Rustlers and the Richmond Sockeyes, tied 2-2 after Red Deer's 7-1 win in Game 4. The game was delayed 30 minutes by four on-ice battles and a fracas behind the Richmond bench in which disorderly fans threw debris including eggs — hitting one Richmond player in the face — along with tomatoes and fish thrown after every Rustler goal. The prior week in Richmond, two Red Deer players were suspended (one and three games) for a post-game altercation with fans, and the Richmond club was fined $500 by the CAHA for failing to provide adequate security.
review.notes: No standalone headline is visible in the OCR; the article begins directly with the byline. The corrected byline includes the full attribution "of The Advocate" as it appears in the OCR. The drafted headline was a paraphrase and has been cleared.
